title: pittosporum cauliflorum f. flocculosum
intro: Pittosporum cauliflorum f. flocculosum, also known as Kohuhu, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of limonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions. In traditional herbal preparations, Kohuhu is often combined with other plants to make teas, infusions, and poultices, but it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
